In this lesson, we're going to talk about the Thank You page gift offer add-on. This is a special add-on offer that comes all the way at the end of the transaction maximization sequence. So you can see, just as a quick recap, that we send our traffic to our lead magnet offer page. This is where we generate the lead. Then they immediately go to the customer captivation offer page. The individuals that take this offer, then immediately get brought to the reverse add-on, then the soft offer add-on, then the mid-tier add-on, and land on this Thank You page. As you're going to see in a second, this Thank You page has an additional offer. This offer is for some sort of continuity offer, a newsletter, a membership site, etc. But the way it's presented makes this extremely savvy, and the take rate is through the roof. So let's go over the elements of this Thank You page add-on offer. Number one, it's presented on a mock Thank You page. We could really call it a Thank You page. What we're really doing is, after the mid-tier add-on offer, whether they take that or not, they are brought to a Thank You page. This is not to look like the other transaction maximization offer pages. Now we want the customer to feel like the transaction is done. We want them to let their guard down, and we want them to feel like they've reached the end of the sequence, and this is the Thank You page. It needs to look and feel like a Thank You page. It should have a Thank You statement at the top. In this case, we want the customer to feel like the transaction is over. This particular offer gets presented with a VSL with no call-to-action delay. You're going to see that it's not an add-to-card button that you utilize with this offer. This is an offer that is presented as a Thank You gift for new customers. I want you to think about it for one second. A new customer came in, purchased the customer captivation offer, purchased one or more of your transaction maximization offers, maybe all of them, and then landed on this Thank You page. Now there's a video that Auto plays that thanks them for their purchase. Thanks them for becoming a customer. Basically says, as my way of over-delivering value, as my way of showing you how much I appreciate having you now as a new customer, I want to offer you a Thank You gift. Then what gets presented is a free trial, a free membership, but it's not presented as a trial. It's presented as I want to give you a free month in my XYZ membership, or I want to give you a free month to my XYZ newsletter. In essence, it is a trial continuity offer, but it's presented to the customer as a Thank You gift, as a gift. This 30-day trial continuity offer is priced up to $27 to $47 a month. The call to action button has copy that reinforces that this is a gift offer. It doesn't say add to cart. It says, yes, I'll take my free month, or however you need to word it for your particular continuity offer. It's a 14-day trial or that sort of thing. Now, let me be clear on something. Am I saying that at no point we tell them that they will be charged at the end of the month? Absolutely not. You do want to. You do need to. You must be completely 100% upfront with customers that at the end of the month that they will be billed unless they tell you prior. It gets presented as, again, a Thank You gift, as my way of thanking you for trusting in me. I want to give you a gift. I want to give you one month free to XYZ membership site. It's normally $27 a month. What you'll get access to is, boom, feature advantage benefit. Now, you go on to say, at the end of the month, if you love it and you're thrilled with the content, do nothing and you'll be billed at the charter rate of just $27 a month going forward. If for whatever reason you don't want to continue beyond that free month, just shoot me an email. Let me know. Your membership will be canceled and you won't pay anything. Go ahead. Grab your free gift. Enjoy it. Again, it's my way of saying, Thank You. Click the button below that says, Yes, I'll take my free month. What happens is, they actually get a free month and then if they don't cancel, if they don't do anything, they're billed at the end of the month and then ongoing. Again, this is something that can very easily be set up in a variety of different CRM applications. It's very easy to set up in SAM card if that's the route that you choose to go. This is an incredible offer to put at the end of your sequence because of how it's presented. The take rate on this is also through the roof.
